title: Teens Are Turning to Chatbots for Mental Health Help. We Need Rules to Keep Them Safe
gist: RAND senior policy researcher Ryan K. McBain cites new JAMA Pediatrics research showing the share of young people using AI chatbots for mental health advice rose from about 1-in-8 to about 1-in-5 in a single year, and argues chatbots have effectively become a de facto therapist for adolescents, requiring new safety rules.

insights.claim: The share of young people using AI chatbots for mental health advice rose from about 1 in 8 to about 1 in 5 in a single year (more than a 40% increase), per research McBain and colleagues published in JAMA Pediatrics.
insights.speaker: Ryan K. McBain
insights.so_what: Signals a fast-growing, largely unregulated consumer use case for AI among minors — relevant to AI-companion/chatbot platforms' user growth trajectory and to incoming child-safety regulatory risk for those firms.
insights.quote: the share of young people using AI chatbots for mental health advice rose from about 1 in 8 to about 1 in 5 in a single year—more than a 40% increase
insights.locator: In research that my colleagues and I published recently in JAMA Pediatrics

insights.claim: AI chatbots have effectively taken over the therapist's role for a growing share of adolescents even while the broader societal debate over social media's mental-health impact on children remains unresolved.
insights.speaker: Ryan K. McBain
insights.so_what: Reframes AI-chatbot mental-health use as a present-scale phenomenon rather than a speculative future risk, raising the odds of near-term child-safety scrutiny of AI companion/therapy chatbots.
insights.quote: a new technology has taken the therapist's seat: AI chatbots

insights.claim: McBain frames the adoption data as evidence that rules are needed to keep teens safe when they turn to chatbots for mental health help, positioning AI-chatbot safety regulation as the necessary next policy step.
insights.speaker: Ryan K. McBain
insights.so_what: Flags a policy-advocacy angle from a RAND/Harvard-affiliated researcher; worth monitoring for concrete regulatory proposals targeting AI mental-health chatbots aimed at or used by minors.
insights.quote: We Need Rules to Keep Them Safe
